hyperedges.label: Waterfall Elimination Rule Set: parallel, defer, dependencies together prevent sequential async latency
Bundle Lazy Loading Rule Set: dynamic imports, preload, conditional loading together minimize initial bundle impact
useEffectEvent Advanced Pattern Set: effect-event-deps, use-latest, event-handler-refs all address stable callback identity in effects
JavaScript Array Performance Patterns
hyperedges.description: These rules collectively address reducing array iteration cost: combining passes, eliminating intermediate arrays, short-circuiting early, checking lengths before expensive comparisons, and using O(n) single-pass algorithms instead of O(n log n) sort.

hyperedges.label: JavaScript O(1) Lookup Patterns via Map and Set
hyperedges.description: These three rules all use Map (or Set) as the shared data structure to convert O(n) repeated lookups or computations to O(1) — building an index once and querying it cheaply thereafter.

hyperedges.label: Static Content Rendering Optimization Patterns
hyperedges.description: These rules address reducing unnecessary work for static or off-screen content: hoisting static JSX elements, hoisting static RegExp objects, and using CSS content-visibility to skip layout/paint for off-screen items.

hyperedges.label: React Re-render Performance Patterns
React Concurrent Mode Patterns
Device Availability Gate
hyperedges.rationale: All three participate in the single is_available derivation: owner_email IS NULL AND admin_condition='normal' AND sync_condition IS NULL. This three-way joint check is the single source of truth for availability — cannot be expressed as pairwise edges alone.

hyperedges.label: Enterprise Sync Engine Trio
hyperedges.rationale: These three background tasks collectively constitute the enterprise background processing system: only sync_all_enterprises and send_nightly_digest are APScheduler jobs; verify_enterprise_names is a post-import daemon thread. The distinction is a critical design constraint documented across DESIGN.md and DEVELOPMENT.md.

hyperedges.label: Token Expiry Notification Lifecycle
hyperedges.rationale: Enterprise bearer token expiry triggers _emit_token_expired() which creates a token_expired Notification (deduped by unique_together); the notification is deleted on next successful sync. All three nodes participate in this lifecycle which cannot be captured by pairwise edges.

hyperedges.label: Django Production Stack: Framework + Server + Scheduler
Django Expert Skill with Reference Documentation
Resilience Patterns: Circuit Breaker + Retry + Event-Driven

nodes.label: localStorage Versioning and Data Minimization
nodes.rationale: Add version prefix to keys and store only needed fields. Prevents schema conflicts and accidental storage of sensitive data. Try-catch required because getItem/setItem throw in incognito/private browsing, on quota exceeded, or when disabled.
nodes.community_name: Cache Repeated Function Call Results (module-level Map)

nodes.label: Passive Event Listeners for Scrolling Performance
nodes.rationale: Add { passive: true } to touch and wheel event listeners to enable immediate scrolling. Browsers normally wait for listeners to finish to check if preventDefault() is called, causing scroll delay. Do not use passive when preventDefault() is needed.
nodes.community_name: Passive Event Listeners for Scrolling Performance
nodes.norm_label: passive event listeners for scrolling performance

nodes.label: SWR Request Deduplication
nodes.rationale: SWR enables request deduplication, caching, and revalidation across component instances. Multiple component instances share one request rather than each fetching independently. useImmutableSWR for static data, useSWRMutation for mutations.
nodes.community_name: Cache Repeated Function Call Results (module-level Map)

nodes.label: Avoid Layout Thrashing (Batch DOM/CSS reads and writes)
nodes.rationale: Interleaving style writes with layout reads (offsetWidth, getBoundingClientRect, getComputedStyle) forces synchronous browser reflows. Batch all writes first, then read, or batch all reads first, then write. Prefer CSS classes over inline styles for caching and separation of concerns.
nodes.community_name: Avoid Layout Thrashing (Batch DOM/CSS reads and writes)
nodes.norm_label: avoid layout thrashing (batch dom/css reads and writes)

nodes.label: Cache Repeated Function Call Results (module-level Map)
nodes.rationale: Use a module-level Map to cache function results when the same function is called repeatedly with the same inputs during render. Use a Map (not a hook) so it works everywhere: utilities, event handlers, not just React components.
nodes.community_name: Cache Repeated Function Call Results (module-level Map)
nodes.norm_label: cache repeated function call results (module-level map)

nodes.label: Cache Property Access in Loops
nodes.rationale: Cache object property lookups in hot paths to reduce repeated deep property traversals (e.g., obj.config.settings.value) on every iteration. Cache both the value and arr.length before the loop.
nodes.community_name: Cache Repeated Function Call Results (module-level Map)

nodes.label: Cache Storage API Calls (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
nodes.rationale: localStorage, sessionStorage, and document.cookie are synchronous and expensive. Cache reads in memory using a Map. Invalidate cache on storage events (cross-tab changes) and visibilitychange (tab becoming active). Update cache on writes.
nodes.community_name: Cache Repeated Function Call Results (module-level Map)
nodes.norm_label: cache storage api calls (localstorage, sessionstorage, cookies)

nodes.label: Combine Multiple Array Iterations into One Loop
nodes.rationale: Multiple .filter() or .map() calls iterate the array multiple times. Combine into one for-of loop to reduce iterations from N*passes to N.
nodes.community_name: Combine Multiple Array Iterations into One Loop
nodes.norm_label: combine multiple array iterations into one loop

nodes.label: Early Return from Functions
nodes.rationale: Return early when result is determined to skip unnecessary processing. Avoids processing all items even after the answer is known.
nodes.community_name: Early Length Check for Array Comparisons

nodes.label: Use flatMap to Map and Filter in One Pass
nodes.rationale: Chaining .map().filter(Boolean) creates an intermediate array and iterates twice. Use .flatMap() to transform and filter in a single pass, eliminating the intermediate array.
nodes.community_name: Combine Multiple Array Iterations into One Loop
nodes.norm_label: use flatmap to map and filter in one pass

nodes.label: Hoist RegExp Creation Outside Render
nodes.rationale: Don't create RegExp inside render — it creates a new object on every render. Hoist static patterns to module scope. For dynamic patterns (e.g., user query), use useMemo(). Warning: global regex (/g) has mutable lastIndex state.

nodes.label: Build Index Maps for Repeated O(1) Lookups
nodes.rationale: Multiple .find() calls by the same key should use a Map built once (O(n)), giving O(1) per lookup. For 1000 orders x 1000 users: 1M ops to 2K ops.
nodes.community_name: Build Index Maps for Repeated O(1) Lookups
nodes.norm_label: build index maps for repeated o(1) lookups

nodes.label: Early Length Check for Array Comparisons
nodes.rationale: When comparing arrays with expensive operations (sorting, deep equality, serialization), check lengths first. If lengths differ, arrays cannot be equal — avoids the expensive comparison entirely.
nodes.community_name: Early Length Check for Array Comparisons
nodes.norm_label: early length check for array comparisons

nodes.label: Use Loop for Min/Max Instead of Sort
nodes.rationale: Finding min/max only requires a single O(n) pass. Sorting is O(n log n) and wasteful. Math.min/max with spread is fine for small arrays but can throw for very large arrays (~124k+ in Chrome).
nodes.community_name: Early Length Check for Array Comparisons
nodes.norm_label: use loop for min/max instead of sort

nodes.label: Defer Non-Critical Work with requestIdleCallback
nodes.rationale: Use requestIdleCallback() to schedule non-critical work (analytics, localStorage saves, prefetching, lazy init) during browser idle periods, keeping the main thread free for user interactions and animations. Not for user-initiated actions needing immediate feedback or rendering updates.
nodes.community_name: Passive Event Listeners for Scrolling Performance

nodes.label: Use Set/Map for O(1) Membership Lookups
nodes.rationale: Convert arrays to Set/Map for repeated membership checks. Array.includes() is O(n); Set.has() is O(1). Build Set once, then all lookups are O(1).
nodes.community_name: Build Index Maps for Repeated O(1) Lookups
nodes.norm_label: use set/map for o(1) membership lookups

nodes.label: Use toSorted() Instead of sort() for Immutability
nodes.rationale: .sort() mutates the array in place, causing bugs with React state and props. Use .toSorted() to create a new sorted array without mutation. Browser support: Chrome 110+, Safari 16+, Firefox 115+, Node.js 20+. Fallback: [...items].sort(...).
nodes.community_name: Early Length Check for Array Comparisons
nodes.norm_label: use tosorted() instead of sort() for immutability

nodes.label: React Activity Component for Show/Hide State Preservation
nodes.rationale: Use React's <Activity> to preserve state/DOM for expensive components that frequently toggle visibility. Avoids expensive re-renders and state loss compared to conditional rendering with &&.
nodes.community_name: React Activity Component for Show/Hide State Preservation
nodes.norm_label: react activity component for show/hide state preservation

nodes.label: Animate SVG Wrapper div for Hardware Acceleration
nodes.rationale: Many browsers don't have hardware acceleration for CSS3 animations on SVG elements directly. Wrap SVG in a <div> and animate the wrapper to enable hardware acceleration for transform, opacity, translate, scale, rotate.
nodes.community_name: Avoid Layout Thrashing (Batch DOM/CSS reads and writes)
nodes.norm_label: animate svg wrapper div for hardware acceleration

nodes.label: Explicit Conditional Rendering (avoid && with falsy numbers)
nodes.rationale: Use explicit ternary operators (? :) instead of && for conditional rendering when the condition can be 0, NaN, or other falsy values that render as text. {count && <Badge>} renders '0' when count is 0; {count > 0 ? <Badge> : null} does not.
nodes.community_name: React Activity Component for Show/Hide State Preservation
nodes.norm_label: explicit conditional rendering (avoid && with falsy numbers)

nodes.label: CSS content-visibility for Long List Rendering Performance
nodes.rationale: Apply content-visibility: auto to defer off-screen rendering. Use contain-intrinsic-size to give the browser a size hint for layout. For 1000 items, browser skips layout/paint for ~990 off-screen items (10x faster initial render).
nodes.community_name: React Activity Component for Show/Hide State Preservation
nodes.norm_label: css content-visibility for long list rendering performance

nodes.label: Hoist Static JSX Elements Outside Components
nodes.rationale: Extract static JSX outside components to avoid re-creation on every render. Especially helpful for large and static SVG nodes. Note: React Compiler automatically hoists static JSX, making manual hoisting unnecessary when compiler is enabled.
nodes.norm_label: hoist static jsx elements outside components

nodes.label: Prevent Hydration Mismatch Without Flickering
nodes.rationale: Avoids visual flicker and hydration errors by injecting a synchronous inline script that reads localStorage before React hydrates, so the DOM already has the correct value when React takes over. Using useState+useEffect causes a flash of default content; direct localStorage access in render breaks SSR.

nodes.label: Suppress Expected Hydration Mismatches
nodes.rationale: For intentional server/client differences (dates, random IDs, locale formatting), use suppressHydrationWarning on the wrapping element to prevent noisy React warnings. Should not be used to hide real bugs.

nodes.label: React DOM Resource Hints
nodes.rationale: React DOM APIs (prefetchDNS, preconnect, preload, preloadModule, preinit, preinitModule) hint the browser about needed resources. Especially useful in server components to start loading resources before the client receives HTML, reducing load time for critical resources.

nodes.label: Use defer or async on Script Tags
nodes.rationale: Script tags without defer/async block HTML parsing, delaying FCP and TTI. defer downloads in parallel and executes after HTML parsing (order preserved); async downloads in parallel and executes immediately when ready (no order guarantee). In Next.js, prefer next/script with strategy prop.
nodes.norm_label: use defer or async on script tags

nodes.label: Optimize SVG Precision
nodes.rationale: Reducing SVG coordinate precision (e.g. to 1 decimal place) decreases file size. Can be automated with SVGO (npx svgo --precision=1 --multipass).

nodes.label: Use useTransition Over Manual Loading States
nodes.rationale: useTransition provides built-in isPending state, removes the need for manual isLoading state, and correctly resets pending state on errors. New transitions automatically cancel pending ones, keeping UI responsive.
nodes.community_name: Use useTransition Over Manual Loading States
nodes.norm_label: use usetransition over manual loading states

nodes.label: Defer State Reads to Usage Point
nodes.rationale: Avoid subscribing to dynamic state (searchParams, localStorage) if you only read it inside callbacks. Read on demand (e.g. window.location.search) to avoid unnecessary subscriptions and re-renders.
nodes.community_name: Use useTransition Over Manual Loading States
nodes.norm_label: defer state reads to usage point

nodes.label: Narrow Effect Dependencies
nodes.rationale: Specify primitive dependencies instead of objects in useEffect to minimize effect re-runs. For derived boolean state, compute it outside the effect and use the derived value as the dependency so the effect only runs on meaningful transitions.

nodes.label: Calculate Derived State During Rendering
nodes.rationale: If a value can be computed from current props/state, do not store it in state or update it via useEffect. Derive it during render to eliminate extra renders and state drift.

nodes.label: Subscribe to Derived Boolean State
nodes.rationale: Subscribe to derived boolean state (e.g. useMediaQuery) instead of continuous values (e.g. useWindowWidth) to reduce re-render frequency to only meaningful boolean transitions.

nodes.label: Use Functional setState Updates
nodes.rationale: When updating state based on current value, use functional form (setState(curr => ...)) to get stable callback references, prevent stale closures, simplify dependency arrays, and avoid bugs caused by missing dependencies.

nodes.label: Use Lazy State Initialization
nodes.rationale: Pass a function to useState for expensive initial values. Without the function form, the initializer runs on every render even though the value is only used once. Use when computing from localStorage, building data structures, or performing heavy transformations.

nodes.label: Extract Default Non-primitive Parameter to Constant in Memoized Components
nodes.rationale: Default non-primitive values (arrays, objects, functions) in memoized component parameters create new instances on every render, breaking memo() strict equality comparison. Extract to a module-level constant (e.g. const NOOP = () => {}) to restore memoization.
nodes.norm_label: extract default non-primitive parameter to constant in memoized components

nodes.label: Extract to Memoized Components
nodes.rationale: Extract expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns before computation. This skips the expensive computation entirely when the component is not rendered (e.g. when loading=true). Not needed when React Compiler is enabled.

nodes.label: Put Interaction Logic in Event Handlers
nodes.rationale: Side effects triggered by a specific user action should run in the event handler, not modeled as state + effect. The state+effect pattern causes effects to re-run on unrelated state changes and can duplicate the action.
nodes.norm_label: put interaction logic in event handlers

nodes.label: Don't Define Components Inside Components
nodes.rationale: Defining a component inside another component creates a new component type on every render. React sees a different component each time and fully remounts it, destroying all state, DOM, and running effects again. Pass props instead of using closure over parent variables.

nodes.label: Do Not Wrap Simple Primitive Expressions in useMemo
nodes.rationale: When an expression is simple (few logical/arithmetic operators) and yields a primitive (boolean, number, string), useMemo adds overhead from calling the hook and comparing dependencies that exceeds the cost of the expression itself.
nodes.norm_label: do not wrap simple primitive expressions in usememo

nodes.label: Split Combined Hook Computations
nodes.rationale: When a useMemo or useEffect combines independent tasks with different dependencies, split them into separate hooks. A combined hook reruns all tasks when any dependency changes, even those not relevant to the changed value.

nodes.label: Use Transitions for Non-Urgent Updates
nodes.rationale: Mark frequent, non-urgent state updates (e.g. scroll events) as transitions using startTransition to keep the UI responsive. React can deprioritize transition updates and interrupt them for urgent ones.
nodes.community_name: Use useTransition Over Manual Loading States

nodes.label: Use useDeferredValue for Expensive Derived Renders
nodes.rationale: When user input triggers expensive computations, useDeferredValue keeps input responsive by allowing React to prioritize the input update and render the expensive derived result when idle. Wrap the computation in useMemo with the deferred value as dependency.
nodes.community_name: Use useTransition Over Manual Loading States
nodes.norm_label: use usedeferredvalue for expensive derived renders

nodes.label: Use useRef for Transient Values
nodes.rationale: Values that change frequently without needing a re-render (mouse trackers, intervals, transient flags) should be stored in useRef rather than useState. Updating a ref does not trigger a re-render; use direct DOM manipulation for visual updates.
nodes.community_name: Use useTransition Over Manual Loading States

nodes.label: Use after() for Non-Blocking Server Operations
nodes.rationale: Use Next.js after() to schedule work (logging, analytics, notifications, cache invalidation) after the response is sent, preventing side effects from blocking response latency. Works in Server Actions, Route Handlers, and Server Components.
nodes.norm_label: use after() for non-blocking server operations

nodes.label: Server Actions Authentication Rule
nodes.rationale: Server Actions are public endpoints; always verify authentication and authorization inside each action, never rely solely on middleware or page-level guards. Input validation, then authentication, then authorization, then mutation.

nodes.label: Cross-Request LRU Caching Rule
nodes.rationale: React.cache() only deduplicates within a single request. Use LRU cache (lru-cache with TTL) for data shared across sequential requests. Especially effective with Vercel Fluid Compute where concurrent requests share the same function instance.

nodes.label: Per-Request Deduplication with React.cache() Rule
nodes.rationale: Use React.cache() with primitive args (not inline objects) to deduplicate DB queries, auth checks, and heavy computations within a single request. Next.js fetch is auto-deduplicated; React.cache() is needed for other async work.

nodes.label: Avoid Duplicate RSC Prop Serialization Rule
nodes.rationale: RSC serialization deduplicates by object reference not value. Avoid passing transformed arrays (toSorted, filter, map) as separate props from the server; do transformations on the client with useMemo to avoid duplicate serialization.
nodes.community_name: Avoid Duplicate RSC Prop Serialization Rule
nodes.norm_label: avoid duplicate rsc prop serialization rule

nodes.label: Hoist Static I/O to Module Level Rule
nodes.rationale: Load static assets (fonts, logos, config files) at module level so they run once on first import, not on every request. Use Promise.all to await module-level promises inside handlers. Not for per-request, large, or sensitive data.
nodes.community_name: Hoist Static I/O to Module Level Rule
nodes.norm_label: hoist static i/o to module level rule

nodes.label: Avoid Shared Module State for Request Data Rule
nodes.rationale: Mutable module-level variables cause race conditions and cross-request data leaks in concurrent RSC/SSR renders. Keep request-scoped data local to the render tree by passing it as props. Safe exceptions: immutable static assets, correctly-keyed shared caches, process-wide singletons without request-specific state.
nodes.community_name: Hoist Static I/O to Module Level Rule
nodes.norm_label: avoid shared module state for request data rule

nodes.label: Parallel Data Fetching with Component Composition Rule
nodes.rationale: RSC components execute sequentially within a tree. Restructure with composition (sibling async components or children prop pattern) so multiple fetches run simultaneously rather than sequentially, eliminating server-side waterfalls.
nodes.community_name: Parallel Data Fetching with Component Composition Rule
nodes.norm_label: parallel data fetching with component composition rule

nodes.label: Parallel Nested Data Fetching Rule
nodes.rationale: When fetching nested data, chain dependent fetches within each item's promise (e.g. getChat(id).then(chat => getUser(chat.author))) instead of two separate Promise.all calls, so a slow item does not block nested fetches for other items.
nodes.community_name: Parallel Data Fetching with Component Composition Rule

nodes.label: Minimize Serialization at RSC Boundaries Rule
nodes.rationale: Serialized props at RSC boundaries impact page weight and load time. Only pass fields the client component actually uses, not entire fetched objects.
nodes.community_name: Avoid Duplicate RSC Prop Serialization Rule
nodes.norm_label: minimize serialization at rsc boundaries rule

nodes.label: Bump Version GitHub Actions Workflow
nodes.rationale: Triggers on PR merge to main; determines bump type (major/minor/patch) from PR labels; runs scripts/bump_version.sh; commits VERSION, backend/VERSION, frontend/package.json; tags the commit; pushes to main; creates a GitHub Release only for major bumps.

nodes.label: Holocron Device Managing Portal (Design Doc)
nodes.rationale: Master design document covering purpose, tech stack, all data models, API surface, ZedCloud status fetch, enterprise sync engine, identity/auth, reservation flow, device table UI, device conditions, export/import, deployment, and decisions log.

nodes.label: Device (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Core domain entity: id, name, serial_number (unique, immutable), description, cluster_device_name, model FK, cluster_id FK (nullable), team FK (nullable SET_NULL), owner_email, lab FK (PROTECT NOT NULL), location_detail, admin_condition (enum: normal/out_of_order/temporarily_leased/dedicated), sync_condition (nullable enum: missing/needs_recovery), idrac_ip, idrac_username, idrac_password_enc, eve_version, device_connectivity (JSON), status, status_fetched_at, reserved_at, last_purpose_text/by/at cache fields, created_at, updated_at. Derived: is_available = owner_email IS NULL AND admin_condition='normal' AND sync_condition IS NULL.

nodes.label: Enterprise (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Admin-managed ZedCloud enterprise credentials: name, cluster FK (CASCADE), bearer_token_enc (Fernet-encrypted, write-only), zcloud_id, zcloud_username, is_active, name_verified, last_sync_at, last_sync_status (ok/error/token_expired), last_sync_error. unique_together=(name,cluster). Bearer token never returned in API. name_verified resets on token PATCH or import overwrite.

nodes.label: Cluster (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: ZedCloud cluster registry: id, name (unique short name e.g. hummingbird), host (ZedCloud hostname). Pre-seeded: hummingbird, alpha, canary, gmwtus, thor, prod. Only admins can add/delete clusters.

nodes.label: UntrackedDevice (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Devices seen in ZedCloud but not in portal inventory: enterprise FK (CASCADE), zcloud_id, name, serial_number, model (denormalized), run_state, eve_version, device_connectivity (JSON), first_seen_at (set only on creation), last_seen_at. unique_together=(serial_number, enterprise).

nodes.label: Notification (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Admin-facing in-app alerts from sync engine. Kinds: token_expired, sync_error, name_mismatch, enterprise_inactive. unique_together=[('kind','enterprise')] prevents duplicate alerts. token_expired deleted on next successful sync. name_mismatch has inline resolution buttons.

nodes.label: ReservationRequest (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Tracks device reservation requests: device FK, requester_email, requested_at, expires_at (requested_at+3h), status (pending/approved/rejected/expired), token (unique 32-byte hex for email approve/reject links). At most one pending request per device.

nodes.label: DevicePurpose (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Stores last 10 purpose entries per device. Cleared entirely on ownership change. Any user can set; clearing requires owner or admin. Denormalized cache on Device (last_purpose_text/by/at) avoids N+1.

nodes.label: OwnershipHistory (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Append-only audit log: device FK, owner_email (null=available), changed_by, changed_at, reason (device_added/reserved/released/force_assigned/request_approved/request_expired/condition_change). Never deleted. Admin-only via API and UI.

nodes.label: Lab (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: DB-backed lab registry: id, name (unique). Pre-seeded: Bangalore Lab, Bangalore Office Space, Berlin Lab, SanJose Lab, CoreSite Lab, Home Lab. New labs added via Django admin, no code change required.

nodes.label: Team (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: DB-backed team registry: id, name (unique). Pre-seeded: ST, EVE, PLE, SRE, Cloud, IT. New teams added via Django admin.

nodes.label: PortalUser (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Portal user separate from Django auth user: id, name, email (unique identity anchor), team FK (PROTECT nullable), user_type (admin/member). Email stored as {prefix}@zededa.com. No Django session; identified via X-User-Email header.

nodes.label: DeviceModel (Data Model)
nodes.rationale: Hardware model catalog: id, name (unique), customer_partner_name (nullable). Any user can add. Searchable from main device search bar via model__customer_partner_name__icontains.

nodes.label: admin_condition Field (Device Condition Flags)
nodes.rationale: User-controlled field. Values: normal, out_of_order (emails all admins, clears owner), temporarily_leased (clears owner), dedicated (shows team chip, requires device.team set). Stored as snake_case, displayed as title-case. Never written by sync engine. Setting out_of_order/temporarily_leased/dedicated clears owner_email and expires pending requests.

nodes.label: sync_condition Field (Device Condition Flags)
nodes.rationale: Sync-engine-controlled, nullable. Values: missing (device not seen in ZedCloud this cycle), needs_recovery (seen only in SUSPECT run-state across all enterprises). Never writable via API or CSV import. When admin_condition=out_of_order, sync engine clears any stale sync_condition to null instead of setting it.

nodes.label: Enterprise Sync Engine (sync_all_enterprises)
nodes.rationale: Hourly APScheduler job. For each active enterprise: fetches devices from ZedCloud, skips UNPROVISIONED/PROVISIONED states, resolves cross-enterprise conflicts using tier-based priority (_RUN_STATE_TIER dict), applies winning candidates via _apply_inventory_candidate(), marks unseen devices missing. Token-expired enterprises skipped and excluded from missing-mark to prevent false positives. token_expired notification deleted on next successful sync.

nodes.label: Run-State Tier Map (Cross-Enterprise Conflict Resolution)
nodes.source_location: ## Enterprise Sync Engine / Cross-Enterprise Conflict Resolution
nodes.rationale: Priority map for resolving same-serial devices across enterprises. Tier 1 (ONLINE/PREPARING_POWEROFF/PREPARED_POWEROFF) wins over Tier 5 (SUSPECT). Tie-break: earlier first_seen_at on UntrackedDevice. SUSPECT winner triggers needs_recovery instead of normal update. States not in map default to tier 99.

nodes.label: verify_enterprise_names() (Post-Import Background Thread)
nodes.rationale: Not a scheduled job. Called as background daemon thread after any import that creates/updates enterprises. Checks is_active=True, name_verified=False enterprises: verifies state (sets inactive+notification if not ACTIVE), checks name match (name_mismatch notification if differs), sets name_verified=True only in active-and-matched branch.

nodes.label: send_nightly_digest (APScheduler Midnight UTC)
nodes.rationale: Registered APScheduler job (midnight UTC). Sends summary email to all admins listing device condition changes from past 24 hours. No-ops if SMTP not configured.

nodes.label: ZedCloud Status Fetch
nodes.rationale: POST /api/v1/devices/{id}/status/ accepts enterprise_id; backend decrypts bearer_token_enc server-side, calls GET /api/v1/devices/name/{cluster_device_name}/status/info. Parses: serial (minfo.serialNumber primary, hardwareInfo.serialNum fallback), EVE version (swInfo[activated=true].shortVersion), connectivity (netStatusList where up+uplink, field ifName NOT name). Serial mismatch rejects update. 404 sets all fields to Unknown.

nodes.label: Reservation Approval Flow
nodes.rationale: Reserve clicked: if device free, transfer immediately. If owned: create ReservationRequest (3h expiry), email owner with /confirm/{token} link. Owner approves/rejects via token (no login needed). Release while pending auto-approves requester. Admin force-assign bypasses flow. All ownership changes append OwnershipHistory and clear DevicePurpose rows.

nodes.label: Auth Pattern (Header-Based, No Sessions)
nodes.rationale: Every API request includes X-User-Email header from localStorage. Backend reads via get_user_email(). Admin check via is_admin(). permission_classes must be explicit on every view (DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES=[]). SSO upgrade path: gateway injects verified header from JWT, strips client-supplied one — no schema changes needed.

nodes.label: Encryption (Fernet)
nodes.rationale: ENCRYPTION_KEY env var (base64 Fernet key). Encrypted fields: Device.idrac_password_enc, Enterprise.bearer_token_enc. idrac_username stored plaintext. Encrypted blobs never exposed in API responses.

nodes.label: Export / Import (Admin Only)
nodes.rationale: GET /api/v1/admin/export?fmt=csv|json (uses ?fmt= not ?format= to avoid DRF content negotiation). POST /api/v1/admin/import with mode=create_only|update_or_create, upsert key=serial_number. Unknown model/cluster names auto-created. Lab and team must exist. Column headers normalized (strip/lowercase/underscore + alias map). admin_condition normalized to snake_case. sync_condition never read from CSV.

nodes.label: Device Table UI
nodes.rationale: Summary bar reflects currently filtered set. Compact primary row + chevron-expand panel. Columns: chevron (fixed), Name (sortable+condition badge), Serial No, Cluster, Name in Cluster, Team, Lab, Owner, Status, Purpose, Actions (fixed last). Column reordering via drag-and-drop persisted in localStorage. Sort: empty values always last. Expand panel: 3 cards (Identity+Placement, ZedCloud Status+Connectivity, IDRAC+Notes). 5 list states: loading, empty, no-results, load-error, stale.

nodes.label: Deployment (Docker + gunicorn + nginx)
nodes.rationale: Two containers via Docker Compose: backend (Django+gunicorn port 8000 internal) + frontend (Node builds React → nginx serves dist/ + proxies /api/). entrypoint.sh: migrate, collectstatic, loaddata clusters_seed.json, optional demo fixture, export START_SCHEDULER=true immediately before exec gunicorn. START_SCHEDULER must NOT be in .env to avoid spurious starts in management commands.

nodes.label: Development Reference (DEVELOPMENT.md)
nodes.rationale: AI-targeted implementation guide: project layout, auth pattern, encryption pattern, serializer field validation patterns (SlugRelatedField, NullableSlugRelatedField), email pattern, ZedCloud fetch implementation, logging conventions (module-level logger, RequestIDMiddleware, set_request_id for background jobs), choices endpoint, feature checklists for new conditions/endpoints/columns/filters.

nodes.label: Logging & Request ID Pattern
nodes.rationale: Every module declares logger=logging.getLogger(__name__). RequestIDMiddleware assigns UUID4 to every HTTP request via ContextVar. RequestIDFilter stamps it on every LogRecord automatically. Background jobs must call set_request_id(f'prefix-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}') as first line. X-Request-ID echoed in response header. Log file rotated daily, 30-day retention.

nodes.label: APScheduler Double-Start Guard
nodes.rationale: APScheduler starts only under runserver child process (RUN_MAIN=true) or when START_SCHEDULER=true. In Docker, entrypoint.sh exports START_SCHEDULER=true immediately before exec gunicorn — NOT in .env — so management commands (migrate, collectstatic) that run earlier do not inherit it. Only two registered jobs: sync_all_enterprises (1h) and send_nightly_digest (midnight UTC).

nodes.label: services/zedcloud.py (ZedCloud API Client)
nodes.rationale: Module-level httpx.Client(timeout=30) shared across calls. fetch_device_status() parses serial (minfo first), EVE version (activated swInfo), connectivity (ifName field NOT name). fetch_enterprise_self() calls /v1/enterprises/self, returns name/zcloud_id/state. fetch_user_self() calls /v1/users/self, stores username in Enterprise.zcloud_username. Constants: ENTERPRISE_STATE_ACTIVE, _ENTERPRISE_STATE_LABELS, SerialMismatchError.

nodes.label: Permission Classes (utils/permissions.py)
nodes.rationale: DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES=[] makes every undecorated view public. IsPortalUser: any registered email. IsAdminPortalUser: user_type=='admin'. IsOwnerOrAdmin: check_object_permissions. get_user_email() reads X-User-Email header. is_admin() checks user_type. Must be explicit on every view.

nodes.label: Serializer Field Validation Patterns
nodes.rationale: SlugRelatedField for FK fields (lab, team) — accepts/returns name string. NullableSlugRelatedField for nullable team FK — converts '' to None. Always add select_related for FK fields. Filter queries use double-underscore traversal (team__name=team, lab__name=lab). validate_<field> methods for non-blank string constraints.

nodes.label: Frontend Project Layout (src/)
nodes.rationale: api/ (client.ts axios with X-User-Email, devices.ts, users.ts, choices.ts, enterprises.ts, notifications.ts, untracked.ts, deviceModels.ts), context/UserContext.tsx, components/ (DeviceTable, SearchBar, Header, NotificationPanel, modals), pages/ (DevicesPage, UsersPage, ClusterEnterprisesPage, UntrackedDevicesPage, LoginPage, ConfirmReservationPage). TanStack Query cache keys defined.

nodes.label: Condition Style Constants (DeviceTable.tsx)
nodes.rationale: Two separate style record sets: ADMIN_CONDITION_STYLES (out_of_order=red, temporarily_leased=violet, dedicated=blue) and SYNC_CONDITION_STYLES (missing=orange, needs_recovery=yellow). Admin badge shown first; sync badge suppressed when admin_condition=out_of_order. Row border: admin wins over sync.

nodes.label: Holocron README
nodes.rationale: Project overview: lab device management portal for Zededa test teams. Stack: Python 3.12, Django 6, DRF, React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, SQLite/PostgreSQL, Docker/nginx, httpx, TanStack Query, APScheduler. Local dev: uv for Python, npm for frontend. Environment variables documented.

nodes.label: Holocron Feature Set (FEATURES.md)
nodes.rationale: Comprehensive feature reference: authentication (identity-picker, localStorage, X-User-Email), device inventory (filters, summary bar, expanded row, per-row actions), device status model (is_available formula, admin_condition flowchart, sync_condition flowchart, combined availability flow), reservation workflow (sequence diagram, release flow), enterprise sync (APScheduler flowchart, adding enterprise, token rotation), untracked devices, user management, notifications (bell + dropdown), bulk import/export, background jobs, REST API summary.

nodes.label: Implementation Plan (PLAN.md)
nodes.rationale: Versioned roadmap: v1 (MVP — shipped: device table, reservation flow, ZedCloud fetch, export/import, conditions), v2 shipped (enterprise sync / auto device sync), v2 next planned (test suite, guest user, OpenAPI, bulk actions, device tags, NLP search, untracked device filter feedback with UntrackedDeviceFlag model), v3 (SSO/LDAP, SMTP wizard), v4 (device capabilities, extended NLP with Quantulum3/spaCy/sentence-transformers/ChromaDB), v5 (infra equipment, mobile, dark mode, optional MCP server).

nodes.label: System Requirements (SYSTEM_REQUIREMENTS.md)
nodes.rationale: IT provisioning guide: VM requirements (min 2 vCPU/2GB RAM for demo; 4 vCPU/8GB for prod), Docker 24+/Compose v2.20+ prerequisites, inbound ports (22/80/443), outbound (SMTP 587, GitHub 443, Docker Hub 443), required secrets (SECRET_KEY, ENCRYPTION_KEY, SMTP vars, PORTAL_BASE_URL, DATABASE_URL), deployment steps, data persistence (sqlite_data and static_files volumes), maintenance commands.

nodes.label: docker-compose.yml
nodes.rationale: Two services: backend (./backend context, env_file=./backend/.env, volumes: sqlite_data:/app/data, static_files:/app/static, ./logs:/app/logs, expose 8000) and frontend (./frontend, ports 80:80, volumes static_files:/static, depends_on backend). Volumes: sqlite_data, static_files.

nodes.label: Auto Device Sync Design Spec
nodes.rationale: Approved design spec for automatic device sync feature. Problem: per-user credential entry was friction-heavy. Solution: admin-managed enterprise credentials, hourly background poll, untracked devices page, missing status marking. Documents Enterprise/UntrackedDevice/Notification models, sync algorithm with _SKIPPED_STATES and _RUN_STATE_TIER, token rotation, verify_enterprise_names(), API endpoints, frontend changes (Clusters & Enterprises tab, Untracked Devices page, revised Refresh Status modal), removal of Vault model, edge cases.

nodes.label: Member View Wireframe (wireframes/index.html)
nodes.rationale: Static HTML wireframe for member (non-admin) view of the Devices page. Shows header with notification bell (no badge for members), Devices nav link only (no Users link for non-admin), filter controls (availability toggle, team/lab/condition selects), device table, and bell open/close state via Alpine.js.

nodes.label: Reservation Confirmation Page Wireframe (wireframes/confirm.html)
nodes.rationale: Static wireframe for /confirm/{token} page — approve/reject reservation flow, no auth needed.

nodes.label: Login Page Wireframe (wireframes/login.html)
nodes.rationale: Static wireframe for identity-picker login page — searchable dropdown, no password, stores email in localStorage.

nodes.label: Device Modals Wireframe (wireframes/modals.html)
nodes.rationale: Static wireframe for device-related modals: Add Device, Edit Device, Reserve, Force Assign, Fetch Status, Export/Import, Add Cluster, Add Model, Ownership History.

nodes.label: is_available Derived Property Rule
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth: is_available = (owner_email IS NULL) AND (admin_condition='normal') AND (sync_condition IS NULL). Used by Available/Reserved filter and status badge. No UNAVAILABLE_CONDITIONS tuple — the two-field check is authoritative. Reservation approve gate checks admin_condition!='normal' OR sync_condition is not None.

nodes.label: GET /api/v1/choices/ Endpoint
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for all dropdowns. Returns labs (DB query), teams (DB query), admin_conditions, sync_conditions, enterprises (active only, with cluster_name). Frontend caches with staleTime:Infinity — cleared on full page reload. New labs/teams appear after refresh without code changes.

nodes.label: Auto-Refresh Intervals
nodes.rationale: Device table: 5 min default (DEVICE_LIST_REFRESH_MS env). Notification panel: 30 sec default (NOTIFICATION_REFRESH_MS env). Both served via GET /api/v1/config/ at startup with staleTime:Infinity. Polling pauses when tab hidden (refetchIntervalInBackground:false).

nodes.label: Untracked Devices Page (/untracked-devices)
nodes.rationale: Route /untracked-devices. All untracked devices fetched in single API call, all filtering client-side. Cluster dropdown (first) restricts Enterprise dropdown (second). Table shows serial, ZedCloud name, model, run state, EVE version, enterprise, first/last seen. Move to Inventory (admin only) opens pre-filled dialog.

nodes.label: _emit_token_expired() Helper
nodes.rationale: Shared helper in sync.py for creating token_expired Notification and sending alert email. Must be called from every token-failure path — never inline the get_or_create block. Notification deleted on next successful sync via Notification.objects.filter(kind='token_expired', enterprise=enterprise).delete() in every success branch.

hyperedges.label: Waterfall Elimination Rule Set: parallel, defer, dependencies together prevent sequential async latency
Bundle Lazy Loading Rule Set: dynamic imports, preload, conditional loading together minimize initial bundle impact
useEffectEvent Advanced Pattern Set: effect-event-deps, use-latest, event-handler-refs all address stable callback identity in effects
JavaScript Array Performance Patterns
hyperedges.description: These rules collectively address reducing array iteration cost: combining passes, eliminating intermediate arrays, short-circuiting early, checking lengths before expensive comparisons, and using O(n) single-pass algorithms instead of O(n log n) sort.

hyperedges.label: JavaScript O(1) Lookup Patterns via Map and Set
hyperedges.description: These three rules all use Map (or Set) as the shared data structure to convert O(n) repeated lookups or computations to O(1) — building an index once and querying it cheaply thereafter.

hyperedges.label: Static Content Rendering Optimization Patterns
hyperedges.description: These rules address reducing unnecessary work for static or off-screen content: hoisting static JSX elements, hoisting static RegExp objects, and using CSS content-visibility to skip layout/paint for off-screen items.

hyperedges.label: React Re-render Performance Patterns
React Concurrent Mode Patterns
Device Availability Gate
hyperedges.rationale: All three participate in the single is_available derivation: owner_email IS NULL AND admin_condition='normal' AND sync_condition IS NULL. This three-way joint check is the single source of truth for availability — cannot be expressed as pairwise edges alone.

hyperedges.label: Enterprise Sync Engine Trio
hyperedges.rationale: These three background tasks collectively constitute the enterprise background processing system: only sync_all_enterprises and send_nightly_digest are APScheduler jobs; verify_enterprise_names is a post-import daemon thread. The distinction is a critical design constraint documented across DESIGN.md and DEVELOPMENT.md.

hyperedges.label: Token Expiry Notification Lifecycle
hyperedges.rationale: Enterprise bearer token expiry triggers _emit_token_expired() which creates a token_expired Notification (deduped by unique_together); the notification is deleted on next successful sync. All three nodes participate in this lifecycle which cannot be captured by pairwise edges.
